stories.headline: These are the Palestinians that Israel has killed during Gaza’s ‘ceasefire’
stories.summary: Israel has killed more than 1,000 Palestinians since agreeing to a US-brokered ‘ceasefire’ in its genocidal war.

stories.headline: Iran Deal Leaves Questions: Paggi
stories.summary: Livia Paggi, Senior Managing Director and Head of Political Risk at J.S. Held joined Bloomberg Open Interest to break down President Trump’s Iran deal, warning that while markets welcomed lower oil prices and an open Strait of Hormuz, the agreement leaves major questions unresolved on Iran’s nuclear program, sanctions relief, ballistic missiles and Israel’s role in Lebanon. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: Iran has come out of the war's first chapter stronger than many expected, but its next challenge may be harder, the BBC Persian Service's Amir Azimi writes.

stories.headline: Patients die in Gaza waiting for medical evacuations Israel keeps blocking
stories.summary: Despite having medical referrals to leave Gaza, Palestinians are not allowed to leave.

stories.headline: US Supreme Court narrows law barring drug users from owning guns

stories.headline: Supreme Court Says Some Drug Users Can Possess Guns
stories.summary: The US Supreme Court ruled that the government can’t categorically bar marijuana users from possessing firearms in a decision that expands the Constitution’s Second Amendment. Mike McKee reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Supreme Court Narrows Law Banning Drug Users From Owning Guns
stories.summary: The justices sided with a Texas gun owner who faced criminal charges after admitting to marijuana use argued that a federal gun law violated the Second Amendment.

stories.headline: Before Making a Deal, Trump Demanded Iran’s Surrender. He Got a Surprise.

stories.headline: First Brands Lender Can’t Distribute Cash From Bankruptcy Sales
stories.summary: Aequum Capital Financial II is temporarily barred from distributing any cash it collects from the inventory sales taking place as part of bankrupt auto-parts supplier First Brands’ liquidation, a judge ruled Wednesday.

stories.headline: Pimco Made $2 Billion Bet on Colombia Ahead of First Round Vote
stories.summary: Funds managed by Pacific Investment Management Co. made a $2 billion bet on Colombia’s local government debt last month, ahead of the first round of a presidential election that is likely to reshape the nation’s economic model.

stories.headline: Stocks Join Bonds Higher as US-Iran Deal Sinks Oil

Wall Street staged a comeback that saw stocks climbing as tech shares led gains and oil fell.
Anastasia Amoroso, Chief Investment Strategist: Private Wealth at Partners Group, discusses the day's market moves and what to expect from tech investment optimism. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Fed Proposes Payment Stablecoin Issuer Identification Program

stories.headline: Argentina MSCI Review Holds Key to Foreign Investment Surge

stories.headline: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity
stories.summary: For many Iranians, the question is not whether the deal means victory, but whether it lowers prices and reduces fear of another war.

stories.headline: Cuba tourism collapses as US pressure campaign bites
